title: Agent worker
description: Configure an AGENT_WORKER for agent-loop execution: model and provider identity, agentTools policy, permissions, resources, and AGENT_RUN workstation companionship.
openingSummary: An AGENT_WORKER is the model-backed executor for prompt-rendered agent loops on AGENT_RUN workstations. Set type to AGENT_WORKER, author model identity and tool policy on the worker, and bind the worker name from an agent-run workstation.

howToUse.title: How To Use
howToUse.body: Author a worker with type AGENT_WORKER. Put model and modelProvider on the worker when you need authored identity; authored values always win over operator defaults. Keep the shared system prompt in body. Configure agentTools.policy when the loop needs filesystem tools. Bind the worker from an AGENT_RUN workstation. Runner selection is separate from modelProvider—choose the built-in runner through factory or workstation runner fields. Agent workers are not INFERENCE_WORKER backends: they do not declare operations, and validation rejects capability declarations on AGENT_WORKER.

schemaReference.title: Schema reference
schemaReference.body: The embed below shows the live Worker base definition with AGENT_WORKER overlay applicability. Selected fields are agent-loop exclusive. Excluded fields belong to other WorkerType families and must not appear on this variant. Open the full Factory schema reference for exhaustive field lookup rather than copying contract text into this page.

examples.title: Examples
examples.body: Use the minimal valid example when starting an AGENT_WORKER. The misuse example shows operations on an agent worker—operations belong on INFERENCE_WORKER and fail validation on AGENT_WORKER.

operationalCautions.title: Operational Cautions
operationalCautions.body: Agent dispatches surface stable failure_class values in work and session inspection metadata. Watch for lease denial, model readiness, tool-policy violations, harness failures, and timeout or cancel paths. Keep secrets out of worker bodies. Allowed tool execution records only bounded tool_diagnostics summaries. Do not treat INFERENCE_WORKER or operations as an agent-loop substitute.

limitsAndAssumptions.title: Limits And Assumptions
limitsAndAssumptions.body: This page is the direct /docs/workers/agent surface for AGENT_WORKER authoring. It is not a sync of packaged CLI docs, not a Workstation routing guide, and not the Inference worker full contract. Compatible Workstation companions name the run-time pairing for this WorkerType; they do not redefine AGENT_WORKER fields. Mock workers stay on their own route and schema.

links.companionIntro: AGENT_WORKER requires an AGENT_RUN workstation companion. The workstation binds the worker name and owns step routing, prompts, limits, and outputs.
links.misuseNote: operations is an inference capability field. Validation rejects it on AGENT_WORKER. Use INFERENCE_WORKER for one-shot operations.
links.failureLeaseCause: Managed runtime capacity exhausted — retry later or increase MODEL resource capacity
links.failureModelReadyCause: Managed runtime missing or still loading — pull or wait before retrying
links.failureToolPolicyCause: Tool call denied by agentTools.policy — change policy or workstation prompt expectations
links.failureHarnessCause: Agent loop runtime failure — inspect dispatch details and factory logs
links.failureTimeoutCause: Dispatch or worker timeout exceeded — increase limits or narrow workstation scope
